name: Salies-du-Salat - Montsaunès Loop
name_original: Boucle Salies du Salat - Montsaunes - Salies du Salat
description: Hike an easy 4.2-mile loop near Salies-du-Salat, offering panoramic Pyrenees views and historical sites like the Montsaunès Templar church.
region_intro: Hiking around Salies-Du-Salat offers diverse landscapes in the central Pyrenees, approximately 75 km southwest of Toulouse. The region features rolling piedmont hills, dense forests, and riverine landscapes alongside the Garonne. Hikers can experience panoramic views of the Pyrenees mountains and tranquil settings around the Lac des Isles. Best hiking trails around Salies-Du-Salat The most popular hiking route is Salies-du-Salat - Montsaunès Loop, a 4.2 miles (6.7 km) trail that takes 1 hour 50 minutes to complete. This easy route includes the historical Montsaunès Templar Church. Another top favourite among local hikers is Loop of Saint-Martory, a moderate 9.0 miles (14.5 km) path. This trail offers varied terrain with riverine landscapes and scenic views. Local hikers also love the Hike starting from Saint-Martory, a 8.6 miles (13.9 km) trail leading through forests and hills, often completed in about 3 hours 42 minutes. Hiking around Salies-Du-Salat is defined by Pyrenees mountain views, rolling piedmont hills, and riverine landscapes. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy strolls to more challenging routes. The routes in Salies-Du-Salat are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.0 stars from over 40 reviews. More than 300 hikers have used komoot to explore Salies-Du-Salat's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Salies-du-Salat?
A: There are nearly 50 hiking trails around Salies-du-Salat, offering a diverse range of experiences. This includes 23 easy routes and 25 moderate routes, ensuring options for various fitness levels.

Q: Are there any easy, family-friendly hikes in the area?
A: Yes, Salies-du-Salat offers several easy routes suitable for families. One popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e958397386/salies-du-salat-montsaunes-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salies-du-Salat - Montsaunès Loop&lt;/a&gt;, a 4.2-mile trail that includes the historical Montsaunès Templar Church. Another accessible choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/17681200" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bridge before Saint-Martory loop from Saint-Martory&lt;/a&gt;, which is just over 4 km long.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect to see while hiking near Salies-du-Salat?
A: Hikers in Salies-du-Salat are treated to diverse landscapes, including panoramic views of the Pyrenees mountains, rolling piedmont hills, dense forests, and riverine scenery alongside the Garonne. The area around Figarol, for instance, offers scenic descents and ascents across these varied terrains.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural attractions along the hiking trails?
A: Many trails in the region are dotted with historical and cultural points of interest. You can find sites like the Montsaunès Templar Church, the ruins of the Castle of the Counts of Comminges, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3879768" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Abbaye de Bonnefont&lt;/a&gt;. The town of Saint-Martory also features the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3480950" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Stone Bridge and City Gates&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3480123" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Notre-Dame Church&lt;/a&gt; with its 12th-century Romanesque portal.

Q: Are there any circular walking routes available?
A: Yes, several circular routes are available. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e877446062/loop-of-saint-martory" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Loop of Saint-Martory&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 9-mile circular path offering varied terrain and scenic views. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24668800" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salies-du-Salat loop from Saint-Martory&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Salies-du-Salat?
A: The hiking routes in Salies-du-Salat are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.0 stars from nearly 50 reviews. Hikers often praise the varied terrain, the beautiful Pyrenees mountain views, and the tranquil riverine landscapes.

Q: Is there a lake or water feature that can be visited during a hike?
A: Yes, the Lac des Isles is a notable water feature in the region. It's a man-made lake that provides a tranquil setting and offers beautiful panoramic views, including the silhouette of an old castle. You can find more information about it on the &lt;a href="https://www.hautegaronnetourism.com/activites/lac-des-isles/" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Haute-Garonne Tourism website&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any trails that lead through forests?
A: Many trails in the Salies-du-Salat area traverse dense forests. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e877571586/hike-starting-from-saint-martory" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hike starting from Saint-Martory&lt;/a&gt;, for instance, leads through forests and hills, providing a shaded and immersive natural experience.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Salies-du-Salat?
A: The diverse landscapes of Salies-du-Salat make it suitable for hiking across multiple seasons. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery, while summer provides longer daylight hours. Some trails may also be accessible in winter, though conditions can vary, especially at higher elevations.

Q: Are there any specific viewpoints or scenic spots along the trails?
A: Hikers can enjoy panoramic views of the Pyrenees mountains from various points along the trails. The area around Figarol often provides views of the peak of Cagire, and the Lac des Isles offers picturesque vistas including an old castle silhouette.

Q: What other outdoor activities are available in Salies-du-Salat besides hiking?
A: Beyond hiking, Salies-du-Salat offers opportunities for fishing, canoeing, and mountain biking. The region has also converted old railway lines into cycle paths, providing another way to explore the landscapes. The town is also known as a spa town, famous for its salt water spring.
tips: There is not much left of the chapel, but the bells of the chapel ring every hour on the hour. I was very surprised by that.

faqs.question: Where can I park to start the Loop Salies du Salat - Montsaunes - Salies du Salat hike?
faqs.answer: The trail typically starts in Salies-du-Salat, a spa town known for its salt water springs. You should be able to find parking within the town to begin your hike.

faqs.question: Is this trail suitable for beginners or families with children?
faqs.answer: Yes, this loop is generally classified as easy and presents no technical difficulties. Its modest elevation gain and varied terrain make it suitable for beginners and families looking for an accessible outdoor experience.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this loop?
faqs.answer: You'll encounter varied terrain, including wide dirt paths, shaded earth tracks bordering fields, and some paved road sections. The route features undulating sections with gentle ascents to viewpoints, offering a mix of surfaces.

faqs.question: What historical sites or viewpoints can I see along the way?
faqs.answer: The trail is rich in historical landmarks and scenic views. You can enjoy panoramic views of the Pyrenees from the ruins of the Counts of Comminges' castle and chapel, as well as from the 'Poueigh' where the statue of the Virgin of Montsaunès stands. You'll also pass by the distinctive 12th-century Templar church in Montsaunès, known for its five-bay bell tower and well-preserved frescoes. The starting point, Salies-du-Salat, is also a notable spa town with architectural heritage. You will also pass by the Ruins of the Notre-Dame-de-la-Pitié Church.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this loop?
faqs.answer: This hike is generally recommended as a summer activity. It's best to avoid the trail in snowy conditions, as some sections might become difficult or unsafe. Spring and autumn can also be pleasant, offering milder temperatures and beautiful scenery.

faqs.question: Is the Loop Salies du Salat - Montsaunes - Salies du Salat trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the trail is generally accessible, specific leash rules for dogs are not detailed. It's always advisable to keep your dog on a leash, especially when crossing frequented roads or passing through populated areas, and to respect local regulations regarding pets in natural spaces.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees for this trail?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to hike this loop. It is freely accessible to the public.

faqs.question: Does this trail connect with other paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, sections of this route intersect with other official paths. You will find parts of the Véloroute du Piémont Pyrénéen 05 (Roquefort-sur-Garonne - Saint-Lizier), Route des Navarrines, Rue de la Vieille Église, Ancienne Ligne de Chemin de Fer Boussens - Saint-Girons, and Rue de l'Église along the way.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this hike?
faqs.answer: The estimated hiking time for this loop is approximately 2 hours 40 minutes to 3 hours, depending on your pace and how often you stop to enjoy the views and historical sites.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities or places to eat near the trail?
faqs.answer: The starting point, Salies-du-Salat, is a town with amenities. You can expect to find cafes, restaurants, and other facilities there before or after your hike.

faqs.question: What should I bring for this hike?
faqs.answer: Given the varied terrain and potential for sun exposure, it's recommended to bring comfortable hiking shoes, water, sun protection (hat, sunscreen), and perhaps a light snack. While generally easy, some undulating sections mean proper footwear is beneficial.
